codeReview.summary: The rerun fixes two prior blockers (mutation lookup no longer capped at 500 and filter changes clear stale active reminder state), but the ID-targeting blocker is not fully resolved: reminder mutations still select the first reminder by title/list in AppleScript rather than uniquely targeting by reminder ID.

issues.description: Mutation targeting is still not ID-stable. `_applescript_find_reminder()` builds `first reminder whose name is (...) and ... name of container is (...)`, and `reminder_complete`/`reminder_edit`/`reminder_delete` all use that clause. If multiple incomplete reminders share the same title in the same list, AppleScript can mutate the wrong reminder, so ID-based endpoint semantics remain incorrect for duplicate-title reminders.

The feature contract for this fix includes renaming client methods to `edit_reminder` / `delete_reminder` (`features.json` expectedBehavior), but the client still exposes `reminder_edit` and `reminder_delete` only.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture shared-state documentation for Reminders mutations is stale relative to current implementation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:33 and :151 still describe Reminders mutations as complete/create only, while implementation includes edit/delete plus `reminder_by_id` lookup and list-name disambiguation logic.

summary: Reviewed fix-reminders-correctness (commit 1da45a1), including handoff, transcript skeleton, and diff. The 500-cap lookup bug and stale `active_reminder` bug are fixed, but reminder mutation targeting remains ambiguous for same-title duplicates within a list, so one prior blocking correctness issue persists and the fix review fails.
